title: Leptochilus tricuspis
taxonomy: Leptochilus tricuspis is classified within the family of ferns and more specifically within the genus Leptochilus. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which is the division of vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. This fern is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. It is also known to be a member of the family Polypodiaceae. Leptochilus tricuspis is a species of fern that is endemic to certain regions and is often found in tropical and subtropical areas.

intro: Leptochilus tricuspis, also known as the Chinese dragon's blood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Leptochilus tricuspis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote wound healing.
